title: Compliance log ingestion has stopped
description: Alerts when no Anthropic compliance log events are received within a recent window, indicating the crawler is broken, the Compliance API toggle was disabled, or the Admin API key lost compliance scope.

definition.name: [Anthropic] Compliance log ingestion has stopped
definition.message:
{{#is_alert}}
## What's happening?

No Anthropic compliance log events have been received in the last 1 hour. The Anthropic crawler normally polls every 5 minutes, so an hour of complete silence is a strong signal that compliance log ingestion has stopped.
{{/is_alert}}

{{#is_warning}}
## What's happening?

No Anthropic compliance log events have been received in the last 30 minutes. The Anthropic crawler normally polls every 5 minutes, so this gap may indicate an early sign of an ingestion problem.
{{/is_warning}}

{{#is_recovery}}
## Recovered

Anthropic compliance log ingestion has resumed. Events tagged `source:anthropic service:anthropic.compliance` are flowing again.
{{/is_recovery}}

{{^is_recovery}}

---

## Impact

While compliance logs are not being ingested, your organization loses visibility into Anthropic Console activity (member changes, workspace and API key management, billing actions, and other audit events) — which can create gaps in security review, incident response, and regulatory evidence.

---

## Common causes

* **Compliance API was disabled** — Someone toggled off the Compliance API in the Anthropic Organization settings under Data and privacy.
* **`compliance_logs_enabled` toggle was switched off** — The toggle on the Datadog Anthropic tile was disabled, so the crawler is no longer requesting compliance events.
* **Admin API key was rotated or lost scope** — The Admin API key configured in Datadog was rotated, revoked, or no longer carries the `read:compliance_activities` scope.
* **Anthropic Compliance API outage** — An upstream incident on Anthropic's Compliance API is preventing the crawler from retrieving events.

---

## How to investigate

* Open the [Log Explorer scoped to Anthropic compliance logs](/logs?query=source%3Aanthropic+service%3Aanthropic.compliance) and confirm there is no recent activity.
* Review the **Compliance Logs** section of the Anthropic integration README for setup and troubleshooting steps.
* In the Anthropic Console, go to **Settings -> Data and privacy** and confirm the Compliance API is enabled for the organization.
* On the Datadog Anthropic tile, verify the `compliance_logs_enabled` toggle is on and the configured Admin API key is current.
* Confirm the Admin API key still has the `read:compliance_activities` scope; rotate and re-enter the key in Datadog if necessary.
* Check the [Anthropic status page](https://status.anthropic.com/) for any active Compliance API incidents.

---

## Related links

* [Anthropic integration documentation](https://docs.datadoghq.com/integrations/anthropic/)
* [Log Explorer: Anthropic compliance logs](/logs?query=source%3Aanthropic+service%3Aanthropic.compliance)
* [Anthropic status page](https://status.anthropic.com/)
{{/is_recovery}}
